note: Seed data compiled from papers/abstracts — verify against original papers before citing. Community corrections welcome.

benchmarks.name: GEO-Bench-2
benchmarks.measures: Classification, segmentation, regression, detection, instance seg — grouped into capability groups with a standardized adaptation protocol
benchmarks.finding: No single GeoFM dominates across tasks; EO-pretrained models beat natural-image models on multispectral tasks, but DINOv3-class baselines stay competitive on RGB.

benchmarks.name: PANGAEA
benchmarks.measures: Global multi-domain dense prediction under a frozen-encoder protocol
leaders: varies by domain; FLORO reports strong results with a small pretraining set
benchmarks.finding: Frozen-encoder rankings differ sharply from fine-tuned rankings — adaptation protocol changes who wins.

benchmarks.name: LHRS-Bench
benchmarks.measures: RS understanding & reasoning, 690 multiple-choice questions across capability levels
leaders: LHRS-Bot-Nova (among open RS models at release)
benchmarks.finding: Small (108 images) — treat as a probe, not a ranking; contamination with common RS training sets is a real risk.

benchmarks.name: EarthMind-Bench
benchmarks.measures: Multi-granular (image/region/pixel) + multi-sensor (optical+SAR) understanding, MC and open-ended
benchmarks.finding: SAR subsets crush RGB-trained models: e.g. reported grounding-style scores EarthMind 13.6 vs GeoChat 2.6 (SAR) — the sensor gap is bigger than the model gap.

benchmarks.name: RSMMVP
benchmarks.measures: Vision-centric probes (counting, relative position, small objects) built from CLIP-blind pairs
benchmarks.finding: RS MLLMs fail systematically on spatial/counting probes; the bottleneck is the CLIP-class encoder (Slot A), not the LLM.

benchmarks.name: VRSBench / GeoChat-Bench
benchmarks.measures: Captioning, VQA, visual grounding on RS imagery
benchmarks.finding: Grounding IoU and text metrics diverge — always report both; many instruction sets share source data with these benchmarks (contamination).
